If single-core postprocessing leaves the latter columns as zero, the fused kernel is assuming the full N range is local; add explicit per-core column partition logic or avoid that matmul launch strategy.
After column partitioning, row-wise postprocessing must keep the original global output stride instead of stepping by local column count, or every row after the first reads the wrong slice.
When high-level matmul partitioning keeps breaking fused normalization semantics, replacing it with a direct row-group scalar GEMM path can recover correctness before later optimizing performance.
